revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Araki-jinja, Takayama]][[ja:荒城神社]] {{Expand Japanese|荒城神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Araki Shrine | native_name = 荒城神社 | image = Araki jinja Honden 20121130.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Araki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.218836|137.259426}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Araki Shrine'''|荒城神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hida Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11665887|ltq=Q11665887|qid=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} ** Series ordinal: 5 ** P155: {{ill|Ohotsuno Shrine|lt=Ohotsuno Shrine|qid=Q135039910|1=Ohotsuno Shrine (Q135039910)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039911|ltq=Q135039911|qid=Q135039911|1=Takatano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q622594|ltq=Q622594|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} * {{ill|Hida Province|lt=Hida Province|ckb|پارێزگای ھیدا|cs|Provincie Hida|de|Provinz Hida|en|Hida Province|es|Provincia de Hida|fa|ولایت هیدا|fr|Province de Hida|gl|Provincia de Hida|id|Provinsi Hida|it|Hida (provincia)|ja|飛騨国|ko|히다국|lt|Hidos žemė|nl|Hida (provincie)|pt|Província de Hida|ru|Хида (провинция)|simple|Hida Province|sk|Hida (provincia)|sr|Провинција Хида|su|Propinsi Hida|tr|Hida (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Хіда|zh_min_nan|Hida-kok|zh_yue|飛驒國|zh|飛驒國|qid=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.218836|137.259426}} == image (P18) == * Araki jinja Honden 20121130.jpg == time of earliest written record (P1249) == * {'time': '+0867-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181965 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == heritage designation (P1435) == * {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|lt=Important Cultural Property of Japan|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut Japans|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|es|Bien Cultural Importante de Japón|fi|Japanin tärkeä kulttuuriomaisuus|fr|Bien culturel important|id|Properti Kebudayaan Penting (Jepang)|it|Bene culturale importante (Giappone)|ja|重要文化財|ko|일본의 중요문화재|ro|Patrimoniu cultural important al Japoniei|simple|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|th|ทรัพย์สินทางวัฒนธรรมที่สำคัญ (ญี่ปุ่น)|tr|Önemli Kültürel Varlık (Japonya)|uk|Важливі культурні пам'ятки Японії|zh|重要文化財|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** P518: {{ill|Q2044994|ltq=Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あらきじんじゃ == located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Araki River|lt=Araki River|ceb|Araki Gawa|ja|荒城川|qid=Q20043012|1=Araki River}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8317436 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005010193 == Commons category (P373) == * Araki-jinja, Takayama == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市国府町宮地1386', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Araki-jinja, Takayama}} [[JA:荒城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11618425}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Araki Shrine |Image=[[File:Araki jinja Honden 20121130.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (National Important Cultural Property) |Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} 1386 Miyaji, Kokufu-chō |Coordinates = {{Two-tier wiki coordinates degrees minutes seconds|36|13|7.82|N|137|15|34.19|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity=Ōaraki no Mikoto |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} · former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 7 (Autumn Grand Festival) |Main hall=Three-bay {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Araki Shrine''' (あらきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=listed shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and one of the eight shrines in {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|qid=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The main hall is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/185256 |title=Araki Shrine Main Hall |publisher={{ill|lt=Cultural Heritage Online|Q105237617|qid=Q105237617|1=Cultural Heritage Online}}/{{ill|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|Q1009519|qid=Q1009519|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} |accessdate=2021-06-26}}. The main hall was also recognized in 2016 as part of the {{ill|lt=Japan Heritage|Q18382798|qid=Q18382798|1=Japan Heritage}} designation “The Skills and Spirit of the Hida Craftsmen / Medieval Shrine and Temple Architecture Group of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokufu Basin|1=UNKNOWN}}”{{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story029/ |title=Japan Heritage Portal Site: The Skills and Spirit of the Hida Craftsmen|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2019-02-21}}. At the autumn grand festival held every year on September 7, the “Araki Shrine Kaneuchi and Lion Dance” (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture) is performed{{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/arakijinnjya.html |title=Araki Shrine Kaneuchi Lion Dance |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}}. Since 2016, the autumn grand festival has been held on the first Sunday of September. The area surrounding the shrine is a well-preserved green space mainly composed of {{ill|lt=Japanese cedar|Q147388|qid=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}}, and it has been designated as a Green Environment Conservation Area (designated on September 30, 1977){{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/6062.html|title=List of Green Environment Conservation Areas|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|date=|accessdate=2024-10-19}}. === History === Although the founding date is generally unknown, the {{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|qid=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} states that “its founding dates back to ancient times.” The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} records that on October 5, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japanese era)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}), during the reign of Emperor Seiwa, “Araki no Kami of Hida Province was granted Junior Fifth Rank.” From the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} onward, it was known as Kawahaku Daimyōjin, but during the {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} of the Edo period, investigations determined that it was Araki Shrine. On July 1, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|qid=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a shrine to be visited by the chief priest of the prefectural association ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-7{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=173}}. === Major Cultural Properties === {{Special characters|target=section|description=In “three-bay nagare-zukuri with kokera roofing,” the second character from the back (kokera) uses a character with the wood radical and “market.” It resembles 【柿】 (kaki, persimmon) but is a different character.}} ; {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} (National designation) * Main Hall – The current main hall, designated as a National Important Cultural Property building, was constructed in {{ill|lt=1390|Q6375|qid=Q6375|1=1390}} ({{ill|lt=Genchū|Q1146236|qid=Q1146236|1=Genchū}} 7). This three-bay nagare-zukuri hall with kokera roofing was designated in Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}}) as a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Ancient Shrines and Temples|Q11411552|qid=Q11411552|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law}} (designated as a “National Treasure” under the {{ill|lt=National Treasure Preservation Law|Q11420716|qid=Q11420716|1=National Treasures Preservation Law}}, later reclassified as an “Important Cultural Property” under current law). In Shōwa 38 (1963), seven ridge plaques were additionally designated as attached Important Cultural Properties. ; {{ill|lt=List of Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties|Q11471459|qid=Q11471459|lt=Prefectural Important Cultural Properties|1=list of Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties}} * Araki Shrine deity statues (2 figures){{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/cyoukoku/mokuzouaraki.html |title=Araki Shrine Deity Statues |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}} * Guardian figures (2 figures){{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/cyoukoku/mokuzouzuisinn.html |title=Zuishin |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}} * Waniguchi gong (1) * Masks (4){{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/takayama-mask.html |title=Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}} ; Prefectural Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property * Lion heads (9 items){{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/yukei-minzoku/araki.html |title=Araki Shrine Lion Heads |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}} ; Prefectural Historic Site * Araki Shrine Site – From the shrine grounds, four {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} dwelling remains have been discovered, along with Jōmon {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|qid=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} (Middle to Late Jōmon) and {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|qid=Q479257|1=Stone tool}} (chipped {{ill|lt=stone axes|Q1540412|qid=Q1540412|1=stone axe}}, {{ill|lt=stone arrowheads|Q11587274|qid=Q11587274|1=stone arrowhead}}, polished stone axes, stone swords, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=stone crowns|1=UNKNOWN}}, stone plates, etc.). The site has been designated as a Gifu Prefecture Historic Site under the name “Araki Shrine Site”{{Cite web|Japanese book|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/arakijinnjya_24971.html |title=Araki Shrine Site |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06}}. === Enshrined Deities === * Principal deities ** Ōaraki no Mikoto (13th-generation descendant of {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}) ** Kunimizu Kokuwake no Kami ** Mitsuhanome no Kami * Subsidiary shrines ** Akiba Shrine ** Inari Shrine ** Tsushima Shrine ** Araki Shrine === Shrine Grounds === Image:Araki-jinja 01-Hida Kokufu Hida Takayama.jpg|Front torii gate Image:Araki-jinja Haiden.Hida Kokufu Hida Takayama.jpg|Worship hall Image:Araki-jinja Chinkonsya-Hida Kokufu Hida Takayama.jpg|Chinkon Shrine Image:Araki-jinja Hida Kokufu.jpg|Spacious grounds === Access === * About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Hida-Kokufu Station|Q5357911|qid=Q5357911|1=Hida-Kokufu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * About 15 km (25 minutes by car) from {{ill|lt=Takayama Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11669304|qid=Q11669304|lt=Takayama IC|1=Takayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūbu Jūkan Expressway|Q11367325|qid=Q11367325|1=Chūbu-Jūkan Expressway}} === References === * History of {{ill|lt=Kokufu Town (Gifu Prefecture)|Q6426887|qid=Q6426887|1=Kokufu, Gifu}} / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties volume, etc. * {{Cite book |Japanese book |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|qid=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External Links === {{commonscat|Araki-jinja, Takayama}} * [http://www.gifureki.com/takayama/araki.html Gifu Prefecture: History, Tourism, and Highlights / Takayama City: Araki Shrine] * [http://kankou.city.takayama.lg.jp/2000002/2000026/2000230.html Takayama City Official Tourism Site / Araki Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Araki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Araki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Japan Heritage]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]]
